description: I started out on the ground floor of enterprise retail banking, diving deep into the messy reality of legacy systems. I quickly learned that reliable software isn't just about writing code—it's about rigorous validation and understanding the complex business rules behind every transaction.

As digital transformation accelerated in the UK, I took charge of test automation across mobile and API channels. Navigating the strict regulatory requirements of commercial banking taught me how to balance developer velocity with zero-compromise security.

Scaling SME banking operations required a rethink of our infrastructure. I introduced Docker and microservice-oriented architectures to our testing pipelines, completely decoupling our dependencies and massively reducing release bottlenecks.

Frustrated by data-mining productivity apps, I decided to build my own offline-first Flutter application. Solving complex local-first data synchronization challenges without relying on cloud services was a turning point in my approach to privacy-centric mobile engineering.

Today, I oversee the end-to-end quality of Allica Bank's platforms. By driving large-scale architectural migrations to modern Cypress and Playwright frameworks, I've enabled our engineering teams to ship faster, with absolute confidence in our risk and compliance systems.

Recognizing a gap in family history tools, I architected a multi-platform genealogy ecosystem from scratch. Designing the containerized Node.js backend and orchestrating the entire CI/CD lifecycle sharpened my full-stack platform engineering skills.

Curious about the practical limits of Generative AI, I built an experimental platform to stress-test AI-assisted UI generation. It was a fascinating deep dive into modern React patterns, uncovering both the magic and the immediate limitations of current LLM capabilities.

For my latest mobile product, I wanted to tackle heavy on-device data processing. I engineered a robust Flutter app utilizing Drift/SQLite, pushing the boundaries of what a modern mobile processor can handle locally without compromising user privacy or battery life.
